subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative flow with logical transitions between data collection, model development and evaluations
Well-structured with contents page, numbered subsections and labelled figures/tables
Research question and plan of action are clearly stated, guiding the reader effectively
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and minor logical breaks disrupt flow
Some content (lengthy personal anecdote, repeated explanations) is not mathematically necessary
Several figure captions are terse and a few images are low resolution
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates coherence and a clear structure with mostly relevant content, placing it solidly in band 3. Minor interruptions in logical flow and some unnecessary repetition prevent a band 4 award.

strengths: Multiple representations (tables, scatter plot, model graphs, residual plots) are effectively linked to the discussion
Overall mathematical argument follows a clear logical progression from formula to critique
Data tables are well presented and support subsequent calculations
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (superscripts vs. subscripts) and undefined logistic parameters
Axis labels, units and concise table headings are frequently missing
Typographical and formatting lapses hinder clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the work uses some appropriate language and offers effective representations, persistent notation inconsistencies and missing definitions limit the mathematical communication to band 2.

strengths: Personal narrative and first-person commentary show genuine engagement
Insightful justification for selecting the 2006â2025 period demonstrates independent thinking
weaknesses: Approach largely follows standard syllabus methods with limited creativity
Rationale for selecting specific data points (e.g. x=0,9,19) is not fully explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows clear evidence of a personal approach and significant independent thinking (band 2), but initiative and creativity are not sustained enough to reach the top band.

strengths: Reflection on model limitations and suggestions for alternative metrics (e.g. MAE/MSE)
Thoughtful use of percent error and residual sums to compare model performance
Acknowledgement of external data source (INE projection) for logistic carrying capacity
weaknesses: Depth of critical analysis is limitedâexternal shocks and deeper questioning are not fully explored
Future research implications and comprehensive evaluation beyond 2030 are only briefly mentioned
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis across strands (band 2), but lacks the depth and critical insight required for band 3.

strengths: Appropriate and effective use of linear, quadratic and logistic models throughout
Mathematical techniques are fully commensurate with SL level and correctly applied in derivations
Residual-squared comparisons clearly demonstrate model ranking
weaknesses: Minor arithmetic and typographical errors in the logistic derivation (e.g. incorrect subtraction term)
Occasional notation slip in the slope formula introduces small inaccuracies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration employs relevant mathematics effectively at the SL level (two strands at band 4), with good understanding despite minor errors, justifying a band 4 award.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanatory text proceeds in a logical order: formula â substitution â graph â error table â critique.  Despite a few typographical errors, the mathematical argument is clear to the reader, so communication is âgoodâ rather than exemplary.

Calculations are mostly correct and graphs align with equations.  Nevertheless, the slope formula notation error, rounding slips when solving for âaâ, and an incorrect intermediate logistic arithmetic step show minor inaccuracies, so understanding is good but not thorough.

Tables, scatter plot, three model graphs and residual plots give multiple representations (symbolic, numeric, graphic).  Each is linked to the discussion and allows comparison, fulfilling the âeffective useâ descriptor, though figure quality limits the leap to âhighly effectiveâ.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: data collection, three successive modelling attempts, evaluations, and a final comparison. Transitions between sections are generally smooth and the reader can follow how each model motivates the next.  Minor logical breaks (e.g. digressions on personal travel or brief repetition of modelâdefinitions) occasionally interrupt the flow, preventing the work from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Some initiative is visible in compiling error tables and calculating residual sums, yet the exploration does not move beyond basic syllabus tools or attempt more sophisticated diagnostics.  Creativity is therefore limited.

Analysis includes percentage errors, mean error, and residual sums to compare modelsâshowing thoughtful engagement.  However, parameter estimation errors (e.g. logistic a-value) are not scrutinised, so the analysis is thoughtful but not âdeep and insightfulâ.

The student states that the logistic model is âbestâ and predicts 2030 population, then acknowledges limitations and proposes extensions such as mean absolute error.  This is a clear evaluation with implications, yet not fully comprehensive.

The student reflects on why each model succeeds or fails, notes limitations of ignoring external shocks and suggests alternative error metrics.  This constitutes meaningful reflection but lacks the depth and critical self-questioning required for the top band.

Key symbols (m, b, a, L) are introduced, and equations are typeset.  Nonetheless there are inconsistencies: the slope formula is written with a plus sign, subscripts toggle between 1/0 and 1/2, and logistic parameters are not clearly defined with units.  Such lapses place the work at âsome appropriate language and notationâ.

Personal motivation is clearly stated and the commentary is written in first person, indicating a personal approach.  However, the approach largely follows textbook modelling, so evidence is âclearâ rather than âhighly personal and innovativeâ.

Most material directly supports the modelling task; however explanations of basic concepts (e.g. definition of slope, long biographical motivation) add noticeable length without mathematical necessity.  Repetition in the three evaluation sections also reduces conciseness, so the strand rests at âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ rather than âhighly conciseâ.
Linear, quadratic and logistic functions are all relevant to population modelling and are applied throughout, satisfying the ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ criterion.

The student selects the time span, justifies Spain as a context relevant to future studies and chooses which three functions to test.  This shows noticeable but not outstanding independent thinking; all techniques are standard syllabus methods.

A contents page, numbered subsections and headings for every stage (data, each model, evaluation, limitation, bibliography) demonstrate deliberate structure.  Figures and tables are labelled, though some captions are terse and several figures are low-resolution, which slightly detracts from the otherwise strong organisation.

The chosen mathematics (least-squares slope, simultaneous equations for quadratic coefficients, logistic fitting) is fully commensurate with SL level.  No techniques beyond the course are attempted, so âfully commensurateâ but not âcomprehensive/expertâ.

annotations.comment: The personal anecdote is lengthy and somewhat unrelated to the modelling task; consider condensing or focusing solely on key motivations.
The explorationâs aim is clearly stated, guiding the reader with a precise research question and purpose.
Personal narrative shows genuine engagement and personal connection to the topic, enhancing the explorationâs authenticity.
Table header spans multiple lines, which hampers clarity; consolidate into a single-line heading or use multiline header formatting consistently.
Adding axis labels and units to the scatter plot would strengthen mathematical communication and facilitate interpretation of the data.
The nuage de points graphically displays trends effectively, enabling visual insight into population changes over time.
Notation in the slope formula uses superscripts (yÂ² â yÂ¹) instead of subscripts; revise to $$m=\frac{y_2-y_1}{x_2-x_1}$$ for consistency.
The plan of action outlines data gathering and model development logically, establishing a clear sequence of steps.
The justification for selecting 2006â2025 is insightful, showing independent thinking in choosing a period of significant demographic change.
Consider splitting long paragraphs in the plan of action into bullet points to improve readability and maintain concise structure.
The data table effectively presents population values and the index x, supporting subsequent modelling with clear numeric representation.
When defining b, the parenthetical âlorsque y = x = 0â could confuse readers; clarify that b is the intercept at x=0 corresponding to y(0).
Consider adding mean absolute error or RMSE alongside percent error for a more comprehensive evaluation of model performance.
The rationale for choosing x=0,9,19 for quadratic fitting is not fully explained; provide justification for selecting those specific points.
An arithmetic error appears in the subtraction step (â1710a = 11186872); re-check the elimination process for accuracy.
The quadratic model evaluation graph demonstrates how well the curve fits chosen and unseen data points.
The table caption labels âPopulation PrÃ©dite par le ModÃ¨le LinÃ©aireâ in the quadratic evaluationâupdate to reflect the quadratique model.
Defining parameter B in the logistic model is omitted; explicitly state that B (or b) represents the growth rate constant with units.
The linear model derivation is presented step by step, demonstrating clear mathematical communication.
Evaluation notes that the model fits endpoints exactly but deviates mid-range; this reflection shows thoughtful analysis of model strength and weakness.
The sentence in the evaluation section is cut off mid-thought; ensure full sentences for clear logical flow and avoid abrupt breaks.
The use of percent error tables is effective for comparing model accuracy numerically.
The quadratic function is derived systematically with three data points, demonstrating appropriate algebraic technique at SL level.
Leveraging the INE projection for carrying capacity shows initiative in sourcing realistic parameter values.
In the logistic model a-calculation, a typo appears: â4442831â should read â44422831â in the subtraction, so re-evaluate a.
Reflecting on potential external shocks (e.g., wars, pandemics) in the logistic evaluation would deepen the critical analysis of model limitations.
The residualâsquared comparison table succinctly demonstrates that the logistic model has the smallest error sum, enabling clear model ranking.
Conclusion clearly states that the logistic model is superior and answers the research question, ensuring a cohesive end to the exploration.
Enhance the conclusion by briefly evaluating model applicability beyond 2030 and reflecting on future research directions.
Limitations and extensions section acknowledges model simplifications and suggests MAE/MSE; a logical reflection of evaluation outcomes.
